title: arenaria arenarioides var. spathulata
intro: Arenaria arenarioides var. spathulata, also known as the coastal sandwort, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the coastal regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of phenolic compounds and saponins. The health benefits of Arenaria arenarioides var. spathulata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antioxidant activities. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
